description: NOTE: the internalTargetPrice field is hiddenForRoles ["quality","supplier"] per axiom A17. A Request For Quotation floated for an approved requisition during strategic sourcing (SCOR S1.8). One RFQ with an auto reference links every supplier response back to the requisition. It is sent to at least three prequalified suppliers (services excepted, where 1-2 capable contractors or a single provider is normal). Each supplier gets a unique authenticated link scoping it to its own quote. The internal target price lives on the RFQ as a benchmark but is never transmitted to any supplier.

fields.name: reference
fields.label: RFQ Reference No.
fields.validation: system-generated; unique; immutable; one reference links all supplier responses to the requisition
fields.purpose: The stable key that ties every quotation, the comparison, and the eventual award back to one sourcing event and one requisition; appears on each supplier's invitation so responses normalize to one RFQ.

fields.name: requisitionId
fields.label: Requisition
fields.validation: must reference a FULLY_APPROVED requisition released to sourcing; immutable
fields.purpose: Binds the sourcing event to the originating demand so the requisition identifier travels through RFQ -> quotation -> award -> PO without re-keying; carries the lines, category, and purchaseType the RFQ is built from.

fields.name: invitedSupplierIds
fields.label: Invited Suppliers
fields.validation: each must be ONBOARDED with qualification complete (not PENDING/SUSPENDED/OFFBOARDED); count >= 3 for goods (fewer requires a justification on the comparison); services may invite 1-2 or a single provider
fields.purpose: The prequalified suppliers the RFQ floats to; the >=3 rule is the competitive-tender control that keeps sourcing contestable. Each gets a unique-link form scoped to its own quote.

fields.name: templateVariant
fields.label: Template Variant
fields.validation: one of the set; Import adds customs/incoterm context, Service swaps item freight fields for contract-period fields
fields.purpose: Selects which RFQ and quote form the supplier fills (Import vs Local vs Service), so an import RFQ collects incoterm/customs context and a service RFQ collects contract period/labour instead of freight.

fields.name: lineItems
fields.label: RFQ Line Items
fields.validation: each line {description, quantity > 0, uom, hsCode?, needDate}; copied from the requisition lines; sent to the supplier
fields.purpose: The structured demand the supplier quotes against (description, quantity, UoM, HS code, need date); the line set every quotation line maps to so comparison is line-for-line.

fields.name: paymentTermsRequest
fields.label: Payment Terms Requested
fields.validation: one of the PaymentTerms enum; the buyer's requested terms, the supplier may counter in its quote
fields.purpose: Signals the payment terms the buyer is seeking so quotes arrive on a comparable basis; the supplier's quoted paymentTerms is what actually binds.

fields.name: deliveryTermsRequest
fields.label: Delivery Terms Requested
fields.validation: free text; the requested delivery basis sent to the supplier
fields.purpose: States the delivery basis the buyer wants (place, timing) so suppliers quote against a common delivery expectation.

fields.name: internalTargetPrice
fields.label: Internal Target Price
fields.validation: >= 0; INTERNAL ONLY; never serialized into any supplier-facing payload, email, or RFQ form (hard invariant)
fields.purpose: The buyer's private benchmark that seeds the savings baseline and informs the abnormal-price-spike flag; deliberately never sent to a supplier so it cannot anchor their quote. The one field whose non-exposure is a stated business rule.

fields.name: sentDate
fields.label: Sent Date
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when status moves to SENT
fields.validation: now() when invitations are dispatched; before deadline
fields.purpose: Stamps when the RFQ went out; anchors the response-window SLA and the supplier responsiveness dimension of the scorecard (submit time vs sent time).

fields.name: deadline
fields.label: Response Deadline
fields.validation: date-time; must be after sentDate; quotes submitted after are flagged late on the comparison
fields.purpose: The cut-off by which suppliers must respond; bounds the sourcing window and feeds the responsiveness measure (on-time vs late submission).

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: DRAFT -> SENT (on dispatch) -> RESPONDED (first quote received) -> AWARDED (on award) | CANCELLED
fields.purpose: Tracks where the RFQ is in the sourcing event so the UI shows the right actions (invite, capture quotes, compare, award) and analytics can measure open vs closed sourcing events.

invariants: A1 the requisition identifier the RFQ carries is immutable end to end
A6 award approval reuses the approval engine with SoD (award approver != selecting buyer)
A12 cross-currency quotes are compared on landedCostInBase
A13 invitation dispatch, each response, and the award are audited and broadcast
A16 each RFQ line is awarded independently; the award event groups winning lines by supplier and emits one PurchaseOrder per distinct winning supplier
internalTargetPrice is never transmitted to a supplier (sourcing invariant)
>=3 suppliers invited for goods; services may invite fewer with justification
